title: Philippiamra arancioana
intro:
I couldn't find any information about the plant Philippiamra arancioana. However, based on the similarity in the name, I found information on the plant Philippine Cheesewood (Murraya paniculata) and the plant Murraya arancioana, which is sometimes referred to as Murraya paniculata. 

Here's a paragraph about Murraya paniculata:

Murraya paniculata, also known as Philippine Cheesewood or Orange Jessamine, is a small, evergreen tree native to the Philippines. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which may help to alleviate various health issues, including fever and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Murraya paniculata include flavonoids, coumarins, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Murraya paniculata is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are often used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve mood.
